blocks.content: Kit's street was three blocks west, and they were on it before either of them acknowledged they'd decided to go this way instead of the other way.
His house had the light on above the garage. On the front porch, on the old swing, his mom was reading.
His mom looked up from her book and smiled.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: Daisy, you want to come in? I've got sweet tea.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I should get back. But thank you, Mrs. Jenkins.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: You tell your mama I said hello.
His mom raised an eyebrow at Kit.
His mom went back to her book. Kit and Daisy walked on.
